```{r}
preProc <- preProcess(train_data[, -which(names(train_data) == "Death")], method = c("center", "scale"))
train_data_norm <- train_data
train_data_norm[, -which(names(train_data) == "Death")] <- predict(preProc, train_data[, -which(names(train_data) == "Death")])
test_data_norm <- test_data
test_data_norm[, -which(names(test_data) == "Death")] <- predict(preProc, test_data[, -which(names(test_data) == "Death")])
```


```{r}

ctrl_kfold <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 10,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary
)
```
specifichiamo la soglia per random forest
```{r}
coords(roc_rf, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))
```

```{r}

threshold_opt <- 0.443
```


```{r}
# Random Forest
mtry_grid <- expand.grid(mtry = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10))
rf_model <- train(Death ~ ., data = train_data, method = "rf",
                  trControl = ctrl_kfold, metric = "ROC",
                  tuneGrid = mtry_grid, ntree = 500)
probs_rf <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")
pred_rf <- ifelse(probs_rf[,"Yes"] >= threshold_opt, "Yes", "No")
pred_rf <- factor(pred_rf, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
cm_rf <- confusionMatrix(pred_rf, test_data$Death, positive = "Yes")
print(cm_rf)
roc_rf <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_rf[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
cat("AUC RF", auc(roc_rf), "\n")
```
scegliamo soglia per xgboost

```{r}
coords(roc_xgb, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))
```

```{r}
threshold_opt <- 0.434519
```


```{r}
# XGBoost
xgb_grid <- expand.grid(nrounds = c(100, 200),
                        max_depth = c(3, 6),
                        eta = c(0.01, 0.1, 0.3),
                        gamma = 0,
                        colsample_bytree = 1,
                        min_child_weight = 1,
                        subsample = 1)
xgb_model <- train(Death ~ ., data = train_data, method = "xgbTree",
                   trControl = ctrl_kfold, metric = "ROC",
                   tuneGrid = xgb_grid)
probs_xgb <- predict(xgb_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")
pred_xgb <- ifelse(probs_xgb[,"Yes"] >= threshold_opt, "Yes", "No")
pred_xgb <- factor(pred_xgb, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
cm_xgb <- confusionMatrix(pred_xgb, test_data$Death, positive = "Yes")
print(cm_xgb)
roc_xgb <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_xgb[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
cat("AUC XGBoost", auc(roc_xgb), "\n")
```

```{r}
coords(roc_knn, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))
```

```{r}
threshold_opt <-0.1
```

```{r}
# KNN
knn_model <- train(Death ~ ., data = train_data, method = "knn",
                   trControl = ctrl_kfold, metric = "ROC",
                   tuneGrid = expand.grid(k = c(3, 5, 7, 9)))
probs_knn <- predict(knn_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")

pred_knn <- ifelse(probs_knn[,"Yes"] >= threshold_opt, "Yes", "No")
pred_knn <- factor(pred_knn, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
cm_knn <- confusionMatrix(pred_knn, test_data$Death, positive = "Yes")
print(cm_knn)
roc_knn <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_knn[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
cat("AUC KNN", auc(roc_knn), "\n")
```
```{r}
coords(roc_svm, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))
```

```{r}
threshold_opt <-0.4284482
```

```{r}
# SVM
svm_model <- train(Death ~ ., data = train_data, method = "svmRadial",
                   trControl = ctrl_kfold, metric = "ROC",
                   tuneLength = 5)
probs_svm <- predict(svm_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")
pred_svm <- ifelse(probs_svm[,"Yes"] >= threshold_opt, "Yes", "No")
pred_svm <- factor(pred_svm, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
cm_svm <- confusionMatrix(pred_svm, test_data$Death, positive = "Yes")
print(cm_svm)
oc_svm <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_svm[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
cat("AUC SVM", auc(roc_svm), "\n")
```
```{r}
coords(roc_nn, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))
```

```{r}
threshold_opt <-0.5378535
```

```{r}
# Neural Network
nn_model <- train(Death ~ ., data = train_data_norm, method = "nnet",
                  trControl = ctrl_kfold, metric = "ROC",
                  tuneGrid = expand.grid(size = c(3, 5, 7),
                                         decay = c(0, 0.1, 0.5)),
                  maxit = 200, trace = FALSE)
probs_nn <- predict(nn_model, newdata = test_data_norm, type = "prob")
pred_nn <- ifelse(probs_nn[,"Yes"] >= threshold_opt, "Yes", "No")
pred_nn <- factor(pred_nn, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
cm_nn <- confusionMatrix(pred_nn, test_data$Death, positive = "Yes")
print(cm_nn)
roc_nn <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_nn[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
cat("AUC Neural Network", auc(roc_nn), "\n")
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(pROC)

# Calcola le ROC
roc_rf  <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_rf[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
roc_xgb <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_xgb[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
roc_knn <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_knn[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
roc_svm <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_svm[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")
roc_nn  <- roc(test_data$Death, probs_nn[,"Yes"], levels = c("No", "Yes"), direction = "<")

# Dataframe per ggplot
roc_df_rf <- data.frame(Specificity = rev(roc_rf$specificities),
                        Sensitivity = rev(roc_rf$sensitivities),
                        Model = "RF")

roc_df_xgb <- data.frame(Specificity = rev(roc_xgb$specificities),
                         Sensitivity = rev(roc_xgb$sensitivities),
                         Model = "XGBoost")

roc_df_knn <- data.frame(Specificity = rev(roc_knn$specificities),
                         Sensitivity = rev(roc_knn$sensitivities),
                         Model = "KNN")

roc_df_svm <- data.frame(Specificity = rev(roc_svm$specificities),
                         Sensitivity = rev(roc_svm$sensitivities),
                         Model = "SVM")

roc_df_nn <- data.frame(Specificity = rev(roc_nn$specificities),
                        Sensitivity = rev(roc_nn$sensitivities),
                        Model = "Neural Network")

# Unisci
roc_all <- rbind(roc_df_rf, roc_df_xgb, roc_df_knn, roc_df_svm, roc_df_nn)

# Plot ROC
ggplot(roc_all, aes(x = 1 - Specificity, y = Sensitivity, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_abline(linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +
  labs(
    title = "ROC Curve Comparison (Test Set, Optimal Threshold )",
    x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
    y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("RF" = "blue",
                                "XGBoost" = "red",
                                "KNN" = "orange",
                                "SVM" = "green",
                                "Neural Network" = "purple")) +
  theme_minimal()

```
Dopo l’ottimizzazione delle soglie tramite Youden Index, i modelli hanno mostrato miglioramenti nelle performance. SVM ha ottenuto i risultati più solidi (AUC = 0.70, Accuracy = 82%, Balanced Accuracy ≈ 0.82), seguito da Random Forest e XGBoost (AUC ≈ 0.83 e 0.82, Accuracy = 73-76%). Anche la rete neurale si comporta bene (AUC = 0.79, Specificity = 0.90), mentre il KNN mostra overfitting verso la classe positiva, con specificity nulla e AUC ≈ 0.53. L’ottimizzazione delle soglie ha permesso di bilanciare meglio sensibilità e specificità rispetto al default 0.5.

#Feature ranking
```{r}
# RF
var_imp_rf <- varImp(rf_model)
print(var_imp_rf)
plot(var_imp_rf, main = "Random Forest - Feature Importance")

# XGBoost
var_imp_xgb <- varImp(xgb_model)
print(var_imp_xgb)
plot(var_imp_xgb, main = "XGBoost - Feature Importance")

# SVM
var_imp_svm <- varImp(svm_model)
print(var_imp_svm)
plot(var_imp_svm, main = "SVM - Feature Importance")

# KNN
var_imp_knn <- varImp(knn_model)
print(var_imp_knn)
plot(var_imp_knn, main = "KNN - Feature Importance")

# Neural Network
var_imp_nn <- varImp(nn_model)
print(var_imp_nn)
plot(var_imp_nn, main = "Neural Network - Feature Importance")

```
XGBoost si focalizza su poche feature ad altissimo impatto (soprattutto HGG e Unknown), tralasciando altre variabili.
Random Forest distribuisce l’importanza in modo più equilibrato, mostrando un pattern complesso ma interpretabile.
SVM e KNN producono ranking più piatti, con minore capacità discriminativa, coerente con la natura meno trasparente di questi algoritmi.
Neural Network presenta alcune anomalie, dando rilievo a feature secondarie come Sex, a scapito di altre più clinicamente rilevanti.

Tutti i modelli concordano sull’importanza di alcune variabili chiave:
HGG (tumori gliali ad alto grado),
Operation_type_bin (tipo di intervento chirurgico),
Unknown (istologia non definita),
Duration_of_symptoms_before_diagnosis_days (durata dei sintomi prima della diagnosi).

Queste compaiono sistematicamente nei primi posti nei grafici di Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM, KNN e Neural Network, suggerendo un’associazione forte e robusta con la mortalità.


L’importanza assegnata da tutti i modelli è supportata da razionali clinici:
HGG riflette la maggiore aggressività e letalità dei tumori ad alto grado.
Operation_type_bin implica che resezioni più radicali (GTR) siano legate a una prognosi migliore.
Unknown potrebbe indicare diagnosi incerte o incomplete, spesso correlate a outcome peggiori.
Durata dei sintomi più lunga può rappresentare ritardo diagnostico e trattamento tardivo.
